reasoning: The current value (as of 12-19, which carries into 12-21) is 2.61%, which sits at the higher end of its 2025 range (2.32% to 2.65%). The recent short-term momentum has been relatively flat to slightly up, creating a clash between short-term positive drift and long-term mean reversion downward. The FRED statistical tool projected a 49% chance of an increase for the 1-week horizon, heavily influenced by the seasonal logic and recent flattening. Another major factor pulling the probability of a strict increase below 50% is the non-trivial chance of an exact tie (no change), given that yields are rounded to two decimal places. The tie probability naturally decreases as the forecast horizon expands (from roughly 6% at a 1-week horizon to ~2% at 3 months), which naturally pushes the probability of a strict increase back towards 50%. Thus, starting just below 50% and climbing towards 0.50 reflects both the structural barrier of ties and the standard spread of uncertainty over longer horizons.
